d: The Zeitwerk 142.055 in Honeygold ‘Lumen’ turns one of Lange’s most unconventional creations into something even more atmospheric. Its semi-transparent dial reveals the mechanics behind the jumping numerals, while the luminous display gives the watch an almost architectural glow in low light. Experimental yet deeply disciplined, it shows how technical ingenuity and rare material can transform the Zeitwerk into something truly singular.

The Zeitwerk 140.048 Handwerkskunst elevates digital time display into the realm of decorative art. Its appeal lies not only in the mechanical drama of the jumping hours and minutes, but in the extraordinary hand-finishing that turns every surface into a canvas of craft. It is a Zeitwerk seen through the lens of métiers d’art, where innovation and traditional artistry meet with rare harmony.

The Zeitwerk 143.050 Decimal Strike gives the collection’s digital display an added sense of rhythm, marking the passage of time with a precision that feels both mechanical and theatrical. Rather than relying on visual complexity alone, it uses sound to deepen the experience, turning each interval into part of the watch’s identity. Bold, cerebral, and unmistakably Lange, it remains one of the most original modern chiming watches.

The Saxonia 404.032 Double Split brings extraordinary technical ambition into a case that remains unmistakably elegant. Its true fascination lies in the ability to measure split times to a level few chronographs attempt, transforming the dial into a stage for mechanical choreography rather than mere timing. Despite its complexity, it still carries the calm, balanced character that defines the Saxonia line.

The Datograph Up/Down 405.035 gives one of Lange’s most revered chronographs a darker, more contemporary presence. Its beauty lies in the dialogue between the perfectly composed dial and the mechanical depth beneath it, while the power-reserve display adds another layer of purpose without disturbing the balance. It is a watch collectors admire not only for what it does, but for how completely it defines the modern haute horlogerie chronograph.

Warm and richly expressive, the Datograph Up/Down 405.031 pairs the collection’s celebrated chronograph architecture with a more intimate sense of luxury. The rose-toned case softens the technical sharpness of the layout, allowing the outsize date, chronograph registers, and power reserve to feel harmonious rather than busy. Few watches combine visual authority and mechanical legitimacy with such ease.

The Datograph Perpetual Tourbillon 740.056 gathers three of high watchmaking’s most respected complications into a design that still feels remarkably poised. Rather than overwhelming the eye, its calendar indications, chronograph layout, and hidden tourbillon are resolved with the discipline that defines Lange at its best. It is a watch of immense substance, yet its greatest strength is how effortlessly that complexity is made to feel coherent.

Dark, intricate, and technically formidable, the Datograph Perpetual Tourbillon 740.036 gives grand complication a more modern and commanding tone. The contrast between the bold case presence and the precise symmetry of the dial makes the watch feel both dramatic and deeply controlled. It is a piece that rewards close attention, revealing layer after layer of mechanical intelligence without ever losing composure.

The Lange 1 117.025 distils the collection’s identity into its pure essentials, where the off-centre dial and outsize date still feel as fresh as they did at the model’s debut. Its appeal lies in proportion rather than ornament, with every display positioned in a way that feels deliberate and unmistakably Lange. Quietly distinctive, it is one of the clearest expressions of modern German watch design.

The Lange 1 137.033 gives the collection’s iconic asymmetry a warmer, more intimate character. The rose-toned case enhances the watch’s sense of refinement, while the outsize date and off-centre displays preserve the unmistakable identity that made the Lange 1 a modern classic. It is elegant without conventionality, proving how enduring originality can be when executed with discipline.

The Lange 1 117.032 brings a softer, more classical expression to one of watchmaking’s most recognisable layouts. Its strength lies in the tension between asymmetry and balance, with each display placed off-centre yet resolved into perfect visual harmony. More than a dress watch, it is a study in design confidence, instantly identifiable without ever needing to announce itself.

Created to mark a quarter-century of the Lange 1, the Grand Lange 1 ‘25th Anniversary’ 117.066 celebrates the collection’s defining design language with added ceremonial detail. The familiar off-centre layout and outsize date remain the heart of the watch, but the commemorative touches give it a more reflective, collector-oriented character. It feels less like a variation and more like a tribute to one of modern horology’s great design statements.

The Zeitwerk Date 148.038 expands the collection’s radical digital display without compromising its architectural clarity. The date indication is integrated in a way that feels entirely native to the design, reinforcing the watch’s reputation for solving familiar functions through unconventional engineering. It remains unmistakably Zeitwerk, cerebral, forceful, and unlike anything else in contemporary watchmaking.

The Saxonia Thin 211.087 shows how little is needed when proportion, finish, and material are handled at the highest level. Its restraint is the point: a pared-back dial, slim profile, and quiet case presence that shift the emphasis toward nuance rather than complication. It is a watch of understatement, created for those who value purity over display.

The Saxonia Outsize Date 381.043 takes one of Lange’s signature indications and places it within a cleaner, more distilled framework. The result feels less elaborate than the Lange 1, yet no less characteristic, with the oversized date bringing just enough personality to an otherwise composed and elegant dial. It is discreet, but never anonymous.

The Saxonia Moon Phase 384.031 introduces a more poetic register into the collection’s restrained design language. Its moon-phase and outsize date displays add charm and rhythm without disturbing the watch’s essential calm, creating a piece that feels refined rather than decorative. It is a quietly romantic expression of Saxonia, measured and beautifully resolved.

The Datograph Perpetual 410.038 brings high complication into a format that still feels unmistakably Datograph, preserving the collection’s celebrated chronograph architecture while integrating a full calendar with remarkable clarity. What makes it so impressive is not merely the quantity of information, but the discipline with which it is arranged. It is grand complication handled with collector-level restraint.

With the Saxonia Thin 205.086, simplicity becomes the whole point. Its deep-toned dial and pared-back display create an atmosphere of quiet confidence, allowing the watch’s slimness and finishing to take centre stage. Rather than seeking attention through complication, it makes its impression through calm proportion and a distinctly modern sense of elegance.

The Lange 1 Tourbillon Perpetual Calendar 720.038 combines two of the manufacture’s most impressive complications with the unmistakable asymmetry of the Lange 1. What should feel dense instead appears remarkably lucid, with the perpetual calendar and hidden tourbillon absorbed into a layout that remains balanced and familiar. It is a masterclass in making immense complexity look natural.

The Lange 1 191.039 preserves everything that made the original so revolutionary, from the off-centre dial to the oversized date, while presenting it with a cooler, more contemporary restraint. Its appeal lies in the confidence of the design itself, where asymmetry becomes harmony through sheer precision of proportion. It is one of those watches that feels inevitable once seen, yet unlike anything else beside it.

The Datograph 403.031 presents Lange’s landmark chronograph in a warmer, more classical light, where the rose-toned case adds depth to an already iconic dial architecture. Its fame rests not simply on the movement, but on the total composition, outsize date, perfectly weighted subdials, and a level of mechanical legitimacy few chronographs can match. It remains one of modern watchmaking’s defining references.

The Datograph 403.032 offers the same revered chronograph architecture in a cooler, more restrained expression, allowing the symmetry of the dial and the outsize date to speak with exceptional clarity. It is a watch that has earned its reputation through substance rather than mythology, celebrated for a movement and layout that still set the standard for the category.

Dark and highly focused, the Datograph 403.035 gives the collection’s celebrated chronograph layout a stronger, more contemporary presence. The black dial sharpens every display and reinforces the watch’s technical character, yet the overall impression remains refined rather than aggressive. It is a Datograph with a little more edge, without sacrificing any of its collector appeal.

The Zeitwerk 140.029 presents Lange’s mechanical digital display in its purest and most forceful form. Its jumping numerals, time bridge, and architectural dial structure make the watch feel less like a conventional dress piece and more like a feat of engineering worn on the wrist. Even years after its debut, it remains one of the boldest reimaginings of time display in modern horology.

Warm precious metal gives the Zeitwerk 140.032 a more intimate tone, but nothing softens the intellectual strength of its design. The jumping digital display and bridge-like architecture still dominate the dial, creating an identity that is immediately recognisable and unlike anything traditional. It is a rare blend of mechanical seriousness and visual originality.

The Lange 1 191.032 expresses the collection’s iconic asymmetry with a more classic warmth, pairing the off-centre dial and outsize date with a softer, more traditional elegance. What endures is the logic of the design, where every display sits apart yet everything feels perfectly ordered. It is one of Lange’s great signatures in its most timeless form.

The Saxonia 216.027 reflects the collection’s philosophy at its most restrained, where clarity, proportion, and finishing matter more than overt complication. Its quiet dial and disciplined case design give the watch a calm confidence that only deepens with time. It is less about display than refinement, a watch that reveals its quality through understatement alone.
